message:
fix(ffs-mvcc): guard WAL truncation against stale checkpoint snapshots (bd-mu0n)

Prevent data loss when truncate_wal() is called after commits have landed
since the last checkpoint:

- truncate_wal() now checks checkpoint_commit_seq against current commit
  sequence and refuses truncation when the checkpoint is stale, returning
  a descriptive FfsError::Format error
- checkpoint() holds the store read lock through both snapshot collection
  AND stats.checkpoint_commit_seq update, closing the race window where a
  concurrent commit() could advance the store between snapshot and stats
- truncate_wal() holds a shared store lock during the freshness check and
  WAL rewrite to block concurrent commits from sneaking in
- WAL last_commit_seq is preserved (set to current_commit_seq) instead of
  being reset to 0 after truncation
- Add #[expect(dead_code)] for set_btrfs_super_total_bytes test helper
- Add two regression tests validating stale-checkpoint rejection and
  post-checkpoint commit preservation across reopen cycles

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
